title: Feature Request battery protection for repeaters

messages.author: Maveric
messages.text: 2 voltage set points are configured voltage set point is for low voltage voltage set point for return to normal operation When the voltage drops below the low voltage setpoint value the repeater will disable repeat set repeat off When the voltage rises above the normal operation voltage setpoint the repeater enables repeat set repeat on This is meant to help protect remote repeaters from going offline during bad weather. (edited)
messages.content_html: 2 voltage set points are configured voltage set point is for low voltage voltage set point for return to normal operation When the voltage drops below the low voltage setpoint value the repeater will disable repeat set repeat off When the voltage rises above the normal operation voltage setpoint the repeater enables repeat set repeat on This is meant to help protect remote repeaters from going offline during bad weather. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 10

messages.author: TKW1/Seagull ZL1JFM
messages.text: That would certainly aid proactive mesh maintenance. A strategic repeater going down without warning and days to reach would not inspire confidence ...
messages.content_html: That would certainly aid proactive mesh maintenance. A strategic repeater going down without warning and days to reach would not inspire confidence ...
messages.reply_html:
mcarper
Also sending out a distress message saying that the battery is low would be good

messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: Are there any node firmwares that can be used to connect to something (e.g. mqtt and NodeRed) that can be automated to check battery levels, or for example can battery level be included in adverts for easier automated logging by repeater owners?
messages.content_html: Are there any node firmwares that can be used to connect to something (e.g. mqtt and NodeRed) that can be automated to check battery levels, or for example can battery level be included in adverts for easier automated logging by repeater owners?
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: VasiliSk
messages.text: In my custom fw i did also a low power cpu mode until battery is charged enough, also holds radio under reset to decrease power usage
messages.content_html: In my custom fw i did also a low power cpu mode until battery is charged enough, also holds radio under reset to decrease power usage

messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: What would also be useful is a simple low temperature holdoff that prevents charging in really low temperatures, configurable so that where someone adds a temperature sensor to their repeater, a logic output from the node can hold off the charger, but that's a different (albeit related) request I suppose.
messages.content_html: What would also be useful is a simple low temperature holdoff that prevents charging in really low temperatures, configurable so that where someone adds a temperature sensor to their repeater, a logic output from the node can hold off the charger, but that's a different (albeit related) request I suppose.

messages.author: Maveric
messages.text: not possible with most nodes as the charging isn't gated or node controlled
messages.content_html: not possible with most nodes as the charging isn't gated or node controlled
messages.reply_html:
IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
What would also be useful is a simple low temperature holdoff that prevents charging in really low temperatures, configurable so that where someone adds a temperature sensor to their repeater, a logic output from the node can hold off the charger, but that's a different (albeit related) request I suppose.

messages.author: IanT [IT-Orp-S omni]
messages.text: Agreed, I'm seeing a few like me abandon the standard chargers and use TP4056 or similar external boards that have those inputs, but agree as a general rule they're not a standard fixture on boards built for indoor hobby projects.
messages.content_html: Agreed, I'm seeing a few like me abandon the standard chargers and use TP4056 or similar external boards that have those inputs, but agree as a general rule they're not a standard fixture on boards built for indoor hobby projects.
